# `breeze stage claim` generalizes `deploy claim` to command stages: alice
# reserves a stage instance's execution slot ahead of time, bob's stage start on
# that exact instance is rejected while the claim holds, and alice's own stage
# start recognizes and consumes it instead of erroring.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze identity register alice
cp stdout alice.token
exec breeze role assign builder alice --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

exec breeze stage claim release build abc123 --ttl 10m --as alice --token-file alice.token
stdout 'claimed release/build \(abc123\) as alice'

# bob (no claim, no role needed since build has no required_role) is rejected
# while alice's claim is active.
! exec breeze stage start release build abc123 --as bob
stderr 'already claimed by "alice"'

# alice's own stage start recognizes and consumes the claim.
exec breeze stage start release build abc123 --as alice
stdout 'build: succeeded'

# The claim is gone now — inventory is empty.
exec breeze inventory
stdout '\(no resources held\)'

# bob can start a DIFFERENT commit with no interference.
exec breeze stage start release build def456 --as bob
stdout 'build: succeeded'

# Claiming a non-command stage (approval or deploy) is rejected outright.
! exec breeze stage claim release review abc123 --as alice --token-file alice.token
stderr 'not a command stage'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/true"]
  }
  stage "review" {
    type               = "approval"
    required_approvals = 1
    approver_role      = "reviewer"
  }
}
